compressed_prompt:
I am a software engineer with 7 years of experience, and I am looking for a new job. Can you create a resume for me and explain each section?

Create a software engineer resume, section-by-section breakdown.
response:
# Software Engineer Resume: Section-by-Section Breakdown

---

## 📄 THE COMPLETE RESUME

---

**ALEX JOHNSON**
📧 alex.johnson@email.com | 📱 (555) 123-4567 | 🔗 linkedin.com/in/alexjohnson | 💻 github.com/alexjohnson | 📍 San Francisco, CA

---

### PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Results-driven Software Engineer with 7 years of experience designing, developing, and deploying scalable web applications and distributed systems. Proven track record of leading cross-functional teams, reducing system latency by 40%, and delivering high-impact features for millions of users. Passionate about clean code, system design, and mentoring junior developers.

---

### TECHNICAL SKILLS

| Category | Technologies |
|---|---|
| **Languages** | Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Go |
| **Frameworks** | React, Node.js, Django, Spring Boot |
| **Cloud & DevOps** | AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, Terraform |
| **Databases** | PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis |
| **Tools** | Git, JIRA, Jenkins, Datadog, Grafana |

---

### PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

**Senior Software Engineer**
*TechCorp Inc. — San Francisco, CA | March 2021 – Present*

- Led a team of 5 engineers to redesign the payment processing microservice, **reducing transaction failures by 35%** and improving throughput by 50%
- Architected and deployed a real-time notification system serving **2M+ daily active users** using Kafka and WebSockets
- Reduced API response time by **40%** by implementing Redis caching and optimizing database queries
- Mentored 3 junior engineers through code reviews, pair programming, and technical workshops
- Collaborated with product and design teams to deliver 12 major features on schedule

---

**Software Engineer**
*StartupXYZ — San Francisco, CA | June 2019 – February 2021*

- Built and maintained RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express, supporting **500K+ monthly active users**
- Migrated legacy monolithic application to microservices architecture, improving deployment frequency by **3x**
- Implemented automated testing suite achieving **85% code coverage**, reducing production bugs by 60%
- Optimized PostgreSQL queries and indexing strategies, cutting average query time from 800ms to 120ms

---

**Junior Software Engineer**
*Digital Agency Co. — Oakland, CA | August 2017 – May 2019*

- Developed responsive front-end features using React and TypeScript for 10+ client projects
- Integrated third-party APIs including Stripe, Twilio, and Google Maps into web applications
- Participated in Agile sprints, daily standups, and bi-weekly retrospectives
- Wrote technical documentation and unit tests for all delivered features

---

### PROJECTS

**Open Source Contribution — React Performance Library**
*github.com/alexjohnson/react-perf-lib*
- Built a lightweight React library for performance monitoring with **1,200+ GitHub stars**
- Used by 300+ developers across 50+ production applications

**Personal Project — DevTracker App**
*github.com/alexjohnson/devtracker*
- Full-stack productivity app built with React, Node.js, and PostgreSQL
- Deployed on AWS using Docker and automated CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions

---

### EDUCATION

**Bachelor of Science in Computer Science**
*University of California, Berkeley — Berkeley, CA | Graduated May 2017*
- GPA: 3.7/4.0
- Relevant Coursework: Data Structures, Algorithms, Operating Systems, Database Systems

---

### CERTIFICATIONS
- AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate *(2022)*
- Google Cloud Professional Data Engineer *(2023)*

---
---

## 📚 SECTION-BY-SECTION BREAKDOWN

---

### 1️⃣ HEADER / CONTACT INFORMATION

**What it is:**
Your personal branding section at the very top of the resume.

**What to include:**
- ✅ Full name *(make it the largest text on the page)*
- ✅ Professional email address
- ✅ Phone number
- ✅ LinkedIn profile URL
- ✅ GitHub profile URL *(critical for engineers)*
- ✅ City and State *(full address is no longer necessary)*

**Key Tips:**
> - Use a **professional email** — avoid nicknames or old handles
> - **Customize your LinkedIn URL** (linkedin.com/in/yourname)
> - Make sure your **GitHub is active** and shows real projects
> - Only include your city if you are **open to local roles** or willing to relocate

---

### 2️⃣ PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY

**What it is:**
A 3-4 sentence elevator pitch that tells recruiters who you are and why you are valuable — before they read anything else.

**What to include:**
- ✅ Years of experience
- ✅ Core technical strengths
- ✅ One or two measurable achievements
- ✅ What you bring to a team or company

**Key Tips:**
> - Keep it to **3-4 sentences maximum**
> - **Tailor this section** for each job application
> - Lead with your strongest selling point
> - Avoid generic phrases like *"hardworking team player"*
> - Think of it as answering: **"Why should we hire you?"**

**❌ Weak Example:**
*"Experienced software engineer looking for new opportunities to grow and learn."*

**✅ Strong Example:**
*"Results-driven Software Engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable systems for millions of users."*

---

### 3️⃣ TECHNICAL SKILLS

**What it is:**
A structured list of your technical toolkit, organized by category for easy scanning.

**What to include:**
- ✅ Programming languages
- ✅ Frameworks and libraries
- ✅ Cloud platforms and DevOps tools
- ✅ Databases
- ✅ Development tools and methodologies

**Key Tips:**
> - **Only list skills you can actually discuss in an interview**
> - Organize into **clear categories** using a table or grouped list
> - **Match keywords** from the job description — ATS systems scan for these
> - Do NOT rate your skills with bars or stars — it looks unprofessional and is subjective
> - Keep the list **current and relevant** — remove outdated technologies

**Pro Tip:**
> Many companies use **Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS)** that scan for keywords. Mirror the exact language used in the job posting.

---

### 4️⃣ PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

**What it is:**
The most important section of your resume. This is where you prove your value with real accomplishments.

**Structure for each role:**
```
Job Title
Company Name — City, State | Start Date – End Date
• Bullet point achievements
```

**What to include:**
- ✅ Job title, company, location, and dates
- ✅ 4-6 bullet points per role
- ✅ Quantified achievements with real numbers
- ✅ Technologies used in context
- ✅ Leadership and collaboration examples

**The Golden Rule — Use the CAR Formula:**

| Letter | Stands For | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **C** | Challenge | Payment system had high failure rates |
| **A** | Action | Redesigned the microservice architecture |
| **R** | Result | Reduced failures by 35% |

**Key Tips:**
> - **Start every bullet with a strong action verb** (Led, Built, Designed, Reduced, Improved)
> - **Quantify everything possible** — numbers make achievements credible
> - Focus on **impact and outcomes**, not just responsibilities
> - List experience in **reverse chronological order** (most recent first)
> - Keep bullet points to **one or two lines** each

**Strong Action Verbs to Use:**

| Built | Designed | Led | Reduced |
|---|---|---|---|
| Architected | Deployed | Mentored | Improved |
| Optimized | Implemented | Collaborated | Delivered |

**❌ Weak Bullet:**
*"Responsible for working on the backend API."*

**✅ Strong Bullet:**
*"Built and maintained RESTful APIs using Node.js, supporting 500K+ monthly active users."*

---

### 5️⃣ PROJECTS

**What it is:**
A showcase of work you have done outside
